Custom Attribute Types and Database Storage

Virtual Element-Based Custom Attributes

These attributes derive values from virtual CDS elements. The database record stores an exit-class name in the EXIT_CLASS field, which is used to calculate the attribute values.

Text Reference Custom Attributes

This configuration uses two database entries:

  • A primary custom attribute with REF_ATTRIBUTE_NAME pointing to a text reference attribute
  • A text reference custom attribute that provides the actual values
Currency-Based Custom Attributes

This structure requires three database entries arranged hierarchically:

  • Primary custom attribute (references a currency attribute)
  • Currency reference attribute (references a text attribute)
  • Text reference attribute (base values)
Unit of Measure Custom Attributes

Similar to currency attributes, this configuration also uses three entries:

Primary custom attribute (references a unit of measure attribute)

Unit of measure reference attribute (references a text attribute)

Text reference attribute (base values)

Implementation Considerations
Virtual CDS Elements

While virtual CDS elements are technically supported for custom attribute definitions, their use is not recommended for performance and maintenance reasons.

Runtime Behavior
Data Availability and Timing

Custom attribute values are calculated and stored when work items are created. However, not all container elements may be available at creation time, and CDS entity data retrieved at this point may differ from real-time values. Post-execution values and UI-updated attributes may not exist during creation, making them unsuitable for inbox display.

Dynamic Updates

Developers can update (available since ABAP Platform Cloud 2505 / ABAP Platform 2025) custom attributes post-creation using the CL_SWF_UTL_RUN_USR_ATTR_CHG utility class. After populating a local buffer with work items (via append_business_obj, append_workflow, or append_workitems), calling request_custom_attribute_chg asynchronously updates all non-final work items within that workflow

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( )
lo_util->append_workflow( '<workflow id>' ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

or business object.

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( ).

lo_util->append_business_obj( VALUE #( catid  = '<category_id>'
                                       typeid = '<type_id>'
                                       instid = '<business_object_instance_id>' ) ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

Performance recommendations

  • Include texts in CDS entities: Text fields should be incorporated directly into CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions (as shown in the example above). This should be documented.
  • Minimize CDS entities: Keep the number of CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions as small as possible—ideally just one. This should be clearly documented.
  • Push domain and reference table texts to database level: If texts from domain values or reference tables are needed, associate them at the database level by linking text associations to the CDS entity that collects these texts. Refer to `I_WorkflowStatusText` as a reference example.
  • Enable full buffering for text tables: Text tables should be configured for full buffering whenever possible.
  • Avoid CDS Virtual Elements: Virtual elements should not be used for customer attribute definitions. If a virtual element must be used, implement the `GET_CALCULATION_INFO` method to specify which CDS entity elements are required for data calculation.

My inbox

How custom attributes are displayed and consumed depends on the view of My Inbox that is being used.

Master-Detail View

The Master-Detail View is the standard view of My Inbox in ABAP Platform. See further details here: Master-Detail View.

When using some predefined names in the definition of custom attributes, some labels in the My Inbox UI can be replaced with the exposed data.

You can find further details in this documentation: Display Custom Attributes in the List View.

Screenshot.

Since ABAP Platform 2102 and ABAP Platform 2021, when performing a mass action (decision), custom attributes are exposed to the user in the multi-selection view. The table in the multi-selection view can be sorted by the custom attributes and also be personalized.

Screenshot

Expert View

The Expert View offers a tabular representation of the standard and the exposed custom attributes of the workflow tasks. The attributes are displayed as columns that can be personalized, sorted, grouped and filtered.

See further details here: Expert View.

SAP Task Center

For tasks to be available in SAP Task Center via the integration also, the defined custom attributes will be exposed to Task Center and be persisted together with the standard attributes in the cache of the Task Center Service on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

The cache of SAP Task Center task is then used by the UIs (e.g. the Web App and SAP Mobile Start) without having to additionally call the task providers. How the custom attributes are shown depends on the connected UI. 

Container based custom attributes

The second option defining custom attributes is to use a reference to particular elements of the workflow container.
Once the task is created they are stored as name-value-pair in the database. As opposed to the container, which is commonly stored as XML, custom attributes can be read via a simple database select.

Following elements can be used:

  • Plain elements
  • Table of plain elements
  • Object attributes (which are plain elements or a table of plain elements) 

Structure elements are not supported. Only simple fields of the structure can be used. The supported data types are:

C

Character string

N

Character string with digits only

D

Date (YYYYMMDD)

T

Time (HHMMSS)

I

Integer number (4-byte integer with sign)

b

1-byte integer, whole number <= 254

s

2-byte integer, only for length field before LCHR or LRAW

P

Packed number

F

Floating point number to accuracy of 8 bytes

g

Character string with variable length (ABAP type STRING)

 

How custom attributes can be defined depends on the UI in which they should be shown.

Please check these two blogs for more details:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/embellishing-your-approvals-without-coding-my-inbox/ba-p/13323357

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-my-inbox-2-0-sp02-features/ba-p/13228953

Exit based custom attributes for My Inbox via Task Gateway Configuration

In general the definition of custom attributes via the configuration in SAP Business Workflow should be preferred to the configuration within Task Gateway since:

Activating a BAdI implementation for a task definition invalidates the definitions and customizations that were done for that task definition in SAP Business Workflow. By that preventing extensions by customers.

Custom attributes that were defined by means of this BAdI will not be exposed to Task Center.

While strongly discouraged custom attributes can still provided at task instance level by implementing the /IWWRK/BADI_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTR BAdI in the /IWWRK/ES_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE enhancement spot. You can find more information here: Custom Attributes. 

 

History: dynamic columns in SAP Business Workplace

For Business Workflow it is possible to create custom columns.

Up to 12 custom columns can be defined using the transaction SWL1 or the menu path  Tools → Business Workflow → Development → Definition Tools → Worklist Client → Dynamic Columns for Worklist.

the following fields can be edited:

  1. User Name: Relevant user name or ‘*’ to support all users
  2. Task : Relevant task type
  3. Expression: Value that should be displayed in the UI.  As source a container element should be specified via its name enclosed into the ‘&’ character. Please ensure that the container element name is taken from the relevant task type, for example '&NAME&'
  4. Header: Label that should be displayed in the UI.

 

Please check these blogs:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sbwp-customization-tip-for-inbox-customization/ba-p/13304850

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/display-dynamic-columns-for-business-workplace-in-the-uwl/ba-p/1288610

Thanks to all contributors to this features & blog.

entry.title: 通过 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中的公司代码限制增强成本中心安全性
entry.content:
简介

安全和访问控制是ERP系统的基本支柱。随着组织不断发展且数据越来越敏感，对精细权限控制的需求变得至关重要。SAP S/4HANA Cloud 继续发展其权限框架，以满足这些关键业务需求。例如: Authorization on company code level for app Manage Cost Centers F1443A

权限体系结构的一个重要增强是引入基于公司代码的公司代码限制。此新功能代表向前迈出的重要一步，有助于组织更严格地控制可以跨不同业务实体访问和管理成本中心信息的人员。

在此博客中，我们将探索此权限增强、其对系统管理员的影响以及确保此功能在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 即将发布的版本 (2608) 中处于激活状态时实现平稳转换所需的步骤。

场景：了解业务需求

成本中心访问控制的挑战

成本中心是用于在不同部门和业务单位之间分配成本、跟踪费用以及管理财务报表的关键组织结构。在具有多个公司代码的大型组织（代表不同的法律实体或业务部门）中，管理谁可以访问哪些成本中心变得越来越复杂。

考虑以下真实场景：

一家全球制造公司经营多个子公司，每个子公司都有自己的公司代码：

- 公司代码 1000（欧洲分部）

- 公司代码 2000（亚洲分部）

- 公司代码 3000（美洲分部）

每个部门都有自己的成本中心和财务经理。之前，在没有详细的公司代码限制的情况下，有权进行成本中心管理的用户可能会无意中访问所有公司代码的成本中心数据 - 可能会暴露敏感财务信息并产生合规性风险。

解决方案：为成本中心部署公司代码限制

新的基于公司代码的成本中心受限访问功能使管理员能够：

1.限制成本中心可见性：限制用户仅查看与其授权公司代码相关联的成本中心

2.控制修改权限：确保用户只能修改其已分配公司代码中的成本中心主数据

3.加强合规性：满足数据分离和访问控制方面的法规要求

4.改进审计跟踪：创建明确、可审计的记录，提供谁访问哪些成本中心的信息

实施详细信息

  1. 权限要求

要配置和管理公司代码限制，管理员必须具有特定权限：

所需业务目录：

- `SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM` - 此业务目录必须分配给负责身份和访问管理 (IAM) 的管理员

这可确保只有合格的 IAM 专业人员才能修改限制配置并防止对安全设置进行未经授权的更改。

 

  1.  配置步骤

管理员必须采取主动步骤来配置公司代码限制，然后才能成为强制限制。方法如下：

关键配置工具：

- 应用：维护业务角色 (F1492)

- 操作：为管理成本中心主数据的所有业务角色预配置公司代码限制值

配置流程：

1.打开维护业务角色应用 (F1492)

2.选择需要公司代码限制的业务角色

3.导航到限制配置部分

4.为限制分配相应的公司代码值

  1. 保存并激活配置

最佳实践：请在系统的低使用期间执行此配置，并在应用于生产系统之前在非生产环境中全面测试。

  1. 受影响的技术对象

公司代码限制将影响 SAP 系统多个层的访问控制：

CDS 视图（核心数据服务）：

- `I_CostCenter` – 主要成本中心主数据视图

- `I_CostCenterVH` – 成本中心值帮助视图

- `I_CostCenterSTDVH` – 成本中心标准值帮助

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – 成本中心交易过账视图

数据字典值帮助：

- `KOST` – 成本中心的传统值帮助

业务对象接口：

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – 业务对象处理成本中心事务

  1. 系统行为的预期更改

公司代码限制生效后，将发生以下更改：

访问限制：

- 不具备适当公司代码权限的用户将无法再查看已分配公司代码之外的成本中心主数据

- 尝试访问未经授权的成本中心将导致数据不可用

- 涉及成本中心选择的系统操作将根据公司代码权限过滤选项

对用户体验的影响：

- 值帮助下拉列表（F4 帮助）将仅显示已授权的成本中心

- 报表和分析将仅显示与已分配公司代码相关的数据

- 成本中心搜索将返回过滤结果

管理影响：

- 管理员必须确保所有受影响的业务角色都已配置公司代码限制

- 配置限制失败可能导致用户对之前可用数据的访问权限丢失

 配置时间表和建议

 第1阶段：立即操作（当前）

- 检查成本中心管理的当前权限设置

- 识别处理成本中心主数据的所有业务角色

— 审计当前用户访问模式以建立基本数据

 

 第 2 阶段：预配置（升级前）

- 使用维护业务角色应用 (F1492) 配置公司代码限制

- 为每个业务角色分配相应的公司代码值

- 开发/沙盒环境中的测试配置

 

 第 3 阶段：验证和测试

- 与每个公司代码的代表用户执行端到端测试

- 验证用户是否只能访问授权的成本中心

- 测试值帮助、报表和事务访问

— 记录任何权限访问的问题或意外行为

 

 第 4 阶段：部署（发布时）

- 将更新部署到生产系统

- 监控系统性能和用户反馈

- 及时解决任何访问问题

- 维护变更文档

 

有关此增强的附加信息：

- SAP 注释3632368 - Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code - SAP for Me

- SAP 文档: Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code | SAP Help Portal

 结论

成本中心主数据管理中引入公司代码限制是 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 权限功能的很有意义进步。使组织能够基于公司代码分配实施精细访问控制，此功能可以增强安全性、改进合规性并降低意外数据暴露的风险。

 

关键要点：

1.主动操作至关重要：不要等到配置公司代码限制时必须使用此功能。现在使用维护业务角色应用预配置值。

2.需要全面计划：审计当前权限结构，识别受影响的业务角色，并提前计划配置策略。

3.测试至关重要：在部署到生产之前彻底测试非生产环境中的配置，以防止用户访问问题。

4.管理监督：确保具有相应权限 (SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM) 的管理员管理这些配置以维护安全性。

5.持续合规：随着组织结构和用户角色的发展，定期审核和维护公司代码限制。

对于致力于保持强大的安全控制和法规合规性的组织，此增强完全符合身份和访问管理的最佳实践。通过采用结构化、计划良好的实施方法，管理员可以在即将到来的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 版本中强制实施公司代码限制时确保无缝过渡。

 

现在是行动的时候了。立即开始预配置工作，以确保此关键权限增强生效时的业务连续性和安全性。

后续步骤：

- 计划检查当前成本中心权限结构

- 识别需要公司代码限制配置的业务角色

- 规划实施时间表，与即将进行的系统更新保持一致

- 联系 SAP 系统管理员，开始预配置工作

entry.title: Influence Tomorrow’s ERP Technology with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1-2026
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA is transforming the way businesses operate. By delivering intelligent automation, real-time analytics, and seamless integration across processes, it enables organizations to drive efficiency, agility, and innovation at scale. Now, you can help shape the future of enterprise resource planning.

Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is still open, offering SAP customers and partners the unique opportunity to directly influence the evolution of SAP S/4HANA. Collaborate with SAP product teams, share your insights early, and contribute to the development of new features and enhancements.

This is more than just a preview – it’s your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of intelligent ERP solutions.

Registration is open until March 13, 2026. 

Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence 

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • Business Partner and Product Maser in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite

ERP - Cross Topics

  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement

ERP Finance & Risk

  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

ERP Private Cloud

  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • E-Mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

ERP Public Cloud

  • Agentic AI – Analytical Business Insights
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Customer returns user experience
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type: Table Operation

Example name: BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certifications updated to release 2602
entry.content:
We’re excited to share that all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certification exams have now been updated to reflect the features, enhancements, and innovations delivered with the 2602 release.

This update ensures that learners, consultants, and implementation teams continue to validate their skills based on the most current capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What This Means for You

1. Exams Aligned with the Latest Release

All learning journeys, courses, practice systems, and system-based exams have been refreshed to align with the 2602 product release. Learners preparing for an exam can expect content that reflects the newest system behavior, UI updates, functional enhancements, and configuration options.

2. Updated Learning Resources

To support learners in their preparation, corresponding SAP Learning content has also been updated. You’ll now find:

  • Revised learning journeys and course material
  • Updated hands-on exercises where applicable
  • New or updated learning system environments reflecting 2602

3. Smooth Transition for Ongoing Learners

If you were already preparing for a certification, you can continue studying without disruption. Updated materials are available immediately, and exam codes remain unchanged unless otherwise communicated in the individual learning journey.

4. Stay Current with SAP Innovations

SAP’s commitment to continuous enablement ensures that certifications stay relevant and reflect the latest release cycles. By certifying on 2602, you demonstrate knowledge and readiness aligned with SAP’s most recent innovations.

What You Should Do Next

Visit the updated learning journeys to access the updated materials:

    • Applying End-to-End Business Processes in SAP Business Suite
    • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
    • Implementing Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Implementing Sourcing and Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Schedule your certification exam when you’re ready—you’ll now be tested on 2602 content.
